finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_10.md:161; chapters/chapter_10.md:163; chapters/chapter_10.md:167; chapters/chapter_10.md:169; chapters/chapter_10.md:173; chapters/chapter_10.md:175; chapters/chapter_10.md:179; chapters/chapter_10.md:189
finding_results.notes: Replaced single thesis-speech block with evidentiary back-and-forth. Mallick now delivers Paul's specific accusation in two short beats (lines 163, 167). Paul makes the key inferential leap himself: he lists the three evidence items, connects them to three different rooms, and concludes 'Those were three different people' (line 173). Mallick's 'At least' (line 175) expands without lecturing. Paul volunteers hearing people at night (line 179), and Mallick finishes his trailing thought. The 'committee' label is preserved in Mallick's private narration (line 47) but no longer appears as a mic-drop in dialogue. Cut: 'because you loved your friend,' the full house-theory summary, 'A committee that never met' in dialogue, 'scared of the same thing' psychological summary, and 'sad and dumb and very human' moral framing. The bedroom-traffic gap still lands through concrete evidence (line 189).

Replaced 'her mother had raised her to believe you didn't show up empty-handed to a place where people were suffering' with 'that was what she did — somebody died, she cooked something, she brought it.' The new phrasing frames the casserole as Mallick's ingrained professional habit rather than maternal instruction. No mother reference, no 'show up empty-handed' construction. Preserves Mallick's domestic register and matter-of-fact voice. The subsequent reasons for the habit (the gesture, the cooking, the Pyrex-as-badge) remain unchanged.

summary: Two dialogue findings addressed. The reveal scene (CH10_DIALOGUE_001) was reworked from a single diagnostic lecture into a pressure-and-response exchange where Paul supplies the central inference — connecting three blank answers to three different tamperers — and Mallick confirms and extends with concrete evidence. The casserole rationale (CH10_DIALOGUE_002) was rewritten to replace the style-bible-adjacent mother-plus-empty-handed construction with a habit-based phrasing native to Mallick's private register. Both fixes preserve the chapter's engine (Mallick realizes multiple independent tamperers), its state shift (bedroom traffic gap remains the open question), and all must-land beats. No structural, continuity, or downstream changes introduced.
